headlines.title: Liege's Defender Is Captured by Germans
RUSSIAN HOSTS BEGIN ATTACK
headlines.subtitle: Staff Orders General Advance All Along Prussian and Austrian Frontiers-Invaders Claim Initial Victories in Skirmishes-Austrian Defeats Announced by Allies.
headlines.fullText:
LONDON, Aug. 22 .- A dispatch to the Exchange Telegraph Company from St. Petersburg says:
"It is officially announced that the Russian army now is advancing along the entire Austro-German frontier and successfully maintaining the offensive at every point of contact.
In a dispatch from St. Petersburg the correspondent of the Reuter Telegram Company also says the Russian general advance, both in Austria and Hungary is progressing without interruption."
A big cavalry engagement of the northern army on Friday was a severe blow to the Germans in East Prussia. An entire German entrenchment battery was captured. Aviators were throwing bombs on the German entrenchments and military bulidigs.
Three Austrian corps are engaged in the Austro-Servian theater of war. They are wedged in the junction of the Drina and Yadel rivers. In a battle of four days' duration, the Servians captured sixty Austrian guns.
The St. Petersburg correspondent of the Daily Mail, in a dispatch dated Friday, says:
"According to information from high sources, the retreat of the Germans, reported yesterday, was marked by brilliant Russian cavalry exploits. Crack guard regiments hurled themselves with irresistible fury on the foe. The Third squadron of Horse Guards captured a hostile battery. The casualties include many of the most distinguished names in Russian aristocracy."
With the Germans bomharding the forts of Namur and large bodies of their troops continuing to move westward, a contact with the allies must soon be joined. The German army will then for the first time find itself facing its chief opponents and a great battle which will have an enormous effect on the present war probably will ebb and flow for several days before a decision is reached.
The Russian army on the eastern frontier has emerged from the mist and in such force as will soon demand more attention.
News From One Side.
The official press bureau this afternoon issued a statement asking the public to bear in mind that for practical purposes all the news comes from the side of the allies.
The official statement continues;
"Very little news filters in from Germany and Austria by the channels of neutral countries, but sufficient does filter through to indicate that the enemy claims successes in various quarters, both in the western and eastern theaters of war.
"No achievement on either side deserves to be called a victory, The French have gained gratifying successes in Alsace and have opened the way to Colmar, capital of the province. The German advance in Belgium has compelled a portion of the Belgian field artillery to retire on Antwerp
Campaign Still Young.
"In the enstern theater there has been some fighting in which the Austrians are helleved to have gained ground, but the campaign in that quarter can scarcely be said to have commenced. The exaggeration into important triumphs of minor episodes in which the allied forces are alleged to have been worsted is a favorite device of the enemy.
(CONTINUTD ON PAGE TWO.)

headlines.title: JAP MINISTER TO LEAVE BERLIN SUNDAY
headlines.fullText:
WASHINGTON, Aug. 22 .--- The Japanese government today instructed its charge d'affaires in Berlin to leave there at 4 a. m., tomorrow, Berlin time, if an answer was not returned then by Germany to the Japanese ultimatum.
Baron Chinda asked Secretary Bryan to transmit through the American embassy at Berlin a message to the Japanese charge d'affaires, giving him instructions in detail as to the course he is to pursue if no answer is returned by Germany.

headlines.title: COMMITTEE TO SELECT CANDIDATES
headlines.subtitle: Citizens" Convention Takes Steps to Make Slate.
headlines.fullText:
Candidates for city offices in the November primary to be supported in the "citizens" movement, will be named by a committee, despite objections that "the slate is made up already." and that "the ticket is about made."
The committee of thirteen, each ward delegation having selected each member, will submit its ticket to a ratification meeting next Saturday morning at 10 o'clock.
This is the committee that will nominate candidates for city office#:
First Ward-J. D. Huff.
Second Ward-George H. Colvin.
Third Ward-G. K. Jackson.
Fourth Ward-Ike Wynn
Fifth Ward-Durward McDonald
Ninth Ward-H. H. Maloney.
Tenth Ward-E. H. McKinley
Twelfth Ward-W. T. McKinley
Thirteenth Ward-Frank J. Evans
The committee met in executive session immediately after the convention was adjourned and elected George H. Colvin chairman and Ike Wynn secretary. It will hold its next meeting Tuesday at 10 a. m. in the directory room of the American National Bank.
The central committee, of which O. S. Gillasple is chairman. recommended that all persons in sympathy with the "citizens" movement, regardless of party affiliation, be eligible to seats in the convention.
It recommended W. P. MeLean, as permanent chairman, and V. S. Wardlaw as permanent secretary. It recommended inviting all the white citizens, of whatever party, to join in the movement. It recommended that this committee be appointed, nominating one cupdidate for each office, and that a ratification meeting be held next Saturday.
Attack on Report.
G. C. Malessere moved the adoption of the report in toto. Leonard M. Levy immediately objected on the ground that the committee had been appointed by Temporary Chairman McDonald and that meetings in wards that had sent no one to select delegates to the convention had been held.
"The committee," Levy said, "has brought in a report, nothing more nor less than an attempt to run anything like this over this convention."
Chairman McDonald suggested that the report dealing with the order of business be adopted, a credentials committee be appointed.
(CONTINUED ON PAGE TWO.)

headlines.title: DECISION ON TRAFFIC LAW DUE MONDAY
headlines.subtitle: Sharp Debate Marks Hearing Before Commission.
headlines.fullText:
A final verdict on the new traffic ordinance, which has been so much discussed and which has been merely operated on trial, is to be handed down Monday by the city commission.
This intention was announced Saturday, following a meeting of the commission at which citizens were invited to express their opinions regarding the good or bad features. After a serious discussion, lasting two hours, which at times became general, the consensus of opinion among commisioners seemed to be that the ordinance will stand, except for the possibility of changing the near-side stop for street cars. the Jaywalking feature and the time limit out, according to views expressed by members of the commission
Room Is Packed.
The little room where commission meetings are held was packed when Mayor Billam opened the meeting. Delegations representing the city board of health and the Federation of Women's Clubs presented petitions asking that boating, hunting and fishing be prohibited on Lake Worth, and that City Attorney McCart draw an ordinance prohibiting fishing.
After the delegations had filed out, the traffic ordinance was tossed to the crowd. And everyone took a hand in taking it apart to see what it was made of. At the end of the session, the traffic ordinance, torn by unskilled hands there seemed to be enough parts to make two new ordinances.
"Before we get any farther." Commissioner Beach said, "I want to tell you gentlemen this much: the enforcement of the traffic ordinance rests all of its weight on the fire and police commissioner's department. It's up to me now for discussion and after Monday whatever is left in the ordinance will be carried out to the full letter of the law. I've discussed the matter with my men and we are going to be strictly police enforced."
Majority For Ordinance
(CONTINUED ON PAGE TWELVE)

headlines.title: Gallant Belgium Compared With El Paso County, Texas
Policemen Stalk a Line of Chalk That Hikers Walk
headlines.fullText:
I HATE to be a peeler and tread the weary beat for every squealer who thinks he has a kick. He tries to do his duty. nine cases out of ten, and shooty, all bad men. the bobo and the killer, the bad man with a past, from post to pillar, and run them in at last. His life he doesn't reckon when tracking deadly foe; wherever perils beckon the peeler has to go. The cop must be a wizard, a Sherlock in disguise, and know, from A to Izzard, the wicked's tures and lies; and he must be a scorper. to master hoodlums rude; and yet he must be a Chesterfieldan dude. The manners Chesterfieldan he must possess of course, or women take his shield in, and tremble in the force. He may tremble, for he will get the ax. As brave as some lion yet dovelike, mild as any babe a week! I'd hate to be a copper, on feet that throb and ache. the cost of every yawper who has a kick to make.
WALT MASON.
(Copyright, 1914, by the Adams Newspaper Service.)

headlines.title: Machine Guns Are Mounted on Autos; Captives Manacled
headlines.fullText:
By Asociated Press.
PARIS. Aug. 22 .- A local paper, describing the entry of the Germans into Brussels, says a striking feature of the maneuver was the presence with the German forces of a hundred automobiles fitted with machine guns ..
A dispatch from Brussels, published here is to the effect that two Belgian officers, manacled to the stirrups of horsemen were obliged in this manner to follow the cortege through the host of spectators. This spectacle provoked an angry demonstration from the populace, but on the whole, however, the people were very self-possessed.

headlines.title: Poly Fire Hall Catches on Fire; Blaze Put Out
headlines.fullText: Fire, which started in a box of dust on the second floor of the Polytechnic fire hall, had burned a hole in the floor and was spreading rapidly when discovered by V. S. Morris, a baker, at 4 a. m. Saturday. The firemen in the building at the time had no intimation that the fire hall was on fire until the alarm was turned in. Some of the firemen who live near the hall extinguished the fire with buckets of water.

headlines.title: Debris in River Prevents Recovery of Bodies of Two Drowned Near Lake Worth.
headlines.fullText:
Because of the raging torrent and the added peril of barbed wire and brush in the bottom of the stream. divers refused to go down in the West Fork below Lake Worth dam Saturday morning to search for the bodies of H. W. Kendrick, owner of the Owl Drug store, 112 1/2 North Main street, and Oscar Hamm, 1214 Houston, who were drowned there Friday afternoon.
The river bed is filled with building material washed from the new dam. For fear that they will get caught on the wire, the divers refused to go down and make the search.
A hundred people with drag nets and long sticks searched for two or three miles up and down the stream Saturday, but neither body had been found.
The men were drowned about 4 o'clock Friday afternoon. A crowd of people searched until dark and then came to town. With more grappling hooks, they went back to the dam below the dam in the hope of finding the bodies lodged in some of them, but without success.
Below the point where Kendrick went down is a barbed wire fence, submerged with nine or ten feet of water, which extends half way across the widened stream, and it is thought possible that the body may be entangled in this.
It is thought that Hamm's body has been carried down stream in the swift current below the spot where he disappeared.
Divers will go into the stream when the river has fallen. The river was steadily dropping Saturday.
Searchers on the banks and in boats have beat out every drift in the river
(CONTINUED ON FAGE TWO.)

headlines.title: LIEGE COMMANDER TAKEN PRISONER
headlines.fullText:
LONDON, Aug. 22 -- It is officially announced that General Leman, commander of the forts at Liege, has been captured by the Germans and is now a prisoner at Cologne.
How he fell into their hands is not stated. General Leman, who is 62, was until recently the head of the Belgian military school, and is considered an authority on military tactics. He is known as a determined and resourceful commander.

headlines.title: AUSTRIAN LOSS 20,000
headlines.fullText: LONDON. Aug. 22-Official sources confirm the report that the Austrians lost 20.000 men in the fighting on the river Drina and that General Demetrievitch, who commands the forts at Liege, is a prisoner of the Germans and en route for Cologne.
